title: Rogersonanthus quelchii
what_description: Rogersonanthus quelchii is an evergreen shrub endemic to the Western Cape Province of South Africa. It belongs to the family Gentianaceae and is characterized by its small, white or yellowish flowers, which are borne in clusters and have a distinctive shape with a long, tube-like calyx. The plant typically grows to a height of 0.5-1.5 meters, with a spread of up to 1 meter, and has dark green, lance-shaped leaves that are 2-10 centimeters long and 1-3 centimeters wide. The leaves are arranged oppositely on the stem and have a smooth, glabrous surface. The fruit is a capsule that splits into two valves, releasing numerous small seeds.
